wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the SignVue microservices architecture project overview. Explain the project's purpose as a demonstration of modern microservices architecture covering REST APIs, JWT authentication, web UI, RabbitMQ, Consul, Traefik, and multi-container deployment. Document the overall system architecture, key components (Traefik reverse proxy, Consul service discovery, RabbitMQ message broker, auth-service, api-service, worker-service, frontend), and main data flows. Include the technology stack summary, target audience, and use cases. Provide architectural diagrams showing component relationships and deployment topology. Address the project's educational value for understanding distributed systems concepts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for authentication endpoints in the SignVue system. Document the /auth/register POST endpoint for user registration including request body schema (email, password), success response format, and error responses (400 for missing fields, 409 for duplicate email, 500 for server errors). Detail the /auth/login POST endpoint covering authentication flow, JWT token generation with expiration, and response schemas. Document the /auth/verify GET endpoint for token validation including Bearer token header requirements and JWT verification process. Include practical curl examples, client implementation guidelines for storing and sending JWT tokens, error handling patterns, and security considerations for token management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Microservices Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the SignVue microservices architecture overview. Explain the purpose and responsibilities of each microservice: auth-service for authentication and user management, api-service for business logic and session management, worker-service for asynchronous job processing, and frontend for user interface. Document the service boundaries, communication patterns, and how they work together to deliver the complete sign language recognition system. Include architectural diagrams showing service relationships and data flow patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the session management system within the API Service. Detail the CRUD operations for interpretation sessions including creation, retrieval, updating, and deletion workflows. Document the session lifecycle from initiation to completion, including UUID generation, session state tracking, and data persistence patterns. Explain the database schema relationships for sessions and translations, query optimization strategies, and transaction handling. Include examples of session creation requests, retrieval by user ID, and session history management. Address concurrent session handling, session timeout mechanisms, and cleanup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started guide for the SignVue project. Include prerequisites (Docker, Docker Compose), step-by-step installation instructions, and first-run setup procedures. Document the complete deployment workflow from preparation through testing, including environment variable configuration (JWT_SECRET), service startup order, and verification steps. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues. Include access URLs for all services (application, Traefik dashboard, Consul UI, RabbitMQ management), initial account creation and role assignment, and basic usage examples. Address both Docker-based deployment and local development modes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Business Logic Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for business logic endpoints in the SignVue system. Document the /api/sessions endpoints including GET (list sessions with pagination/filtering), POST (create new session), GET by ID (retrieve session details), PUT (update session), and DELETE (remove session) operations. Detail the /api/interpretation-requests endpoint for job submission including request schemas, response formats, and status tracking. Document the /api/stats endpoint for admin statistics including data aggregation, filtering options, and response formats. Include comprehensive request/response schemas, query parameters, authentication requirements, authorization levels (USER vs ADMIN), error handling patterns, and practical examples for each endpoint operation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Infrastructure Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the SignVue infrastructure components. Document the Docker Compose configuration including network setup, service dependencies, and container orchestration. Explain the role of each infrastructure component: Traefik reverse proxy for routing and load balancing, Consul for service discovery and health checking, RabbitMQ for message queuing and asynchronous processing, and PostgreSQL for data persistence. Include configuration details, port mappings, and volume mounts. Address scaling considerations and resource allocation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interpretation Request Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the interpretation request processing workflow. Explain the integration with RabbitMQ for asynchronous job queuing, message serialization patterns, and job distribution mechanisms. Document the request validation process, input sanitization, and error handling strategies. Detail the translation processing pipeline, including data transformation, batch processing capabilities, and result aggregation. Include examples of request payloads, response formats, and processing status tracking. Address retry mechanisms, dead letter queues, and monitoring approaches for job processing reliability.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the SignVue microservices system. Describe the high-level design patterns including microservices architecture, API gateway pattern with Traefik, event-driven architecture with RabbitMQ, and service discovery with Consul. Document component interactions, data flows between auth-service, api-service, worker-service, frontend, and infrastructure components. Explain technical decisions, trade-offs, and constraints. Include infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and deployment topology. Provide system context diagrams and component relationship maps. Address cross-cutting concerns like security, monitoring, and service registration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the SignVue data flow architecture. Document the complete data pathways through the system including authentication flow (frontend → auth-service → database), demo processing flow (frontend → api-service → rabbitmq → worker-service), session management flow, and user registration flow. Explain how data moves between services, the role of message queues in asynchronous processing, and data consistency patterns. Include sequence diagrams showing typical user workflows and system interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for PostgreSQL database integration within the API Service. Document the database connection pooling configuration, migration management, and schema initialization processes. Detail the data models for users, sessions, and translations including primary keys, foreign key relationships, and indexing strategies. Explain query patterns, prepared statement usage, and connection lifecycle management. Include database schema diagrams, migration scripts, and data validation rules. Address connection pooling optimization, query performance tuning, and backup/recovery procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Authentication Service. Detail the JWT-based authentication implementation, user registration and login workflows, password hashing with bcryptjs, and role-based access control (USER/ADMIN). Document all API endpoints (/auth/register, /auth/login, /auth/me), request/response schemas, and authentication middleware. Explain database schema for user management, token generation and validation processes, and security considerations. Include practical examples of authentication flows, error handling patterns, and integration with other services. Address JWT secret management and token refresh mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Discovery Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the SignVue service discovery and routing patterns. Document how Consul enables service registration and health checking, how Traefik acts as an API gateway with automatic service discovery, and how Docker networking facilitates inter-service communication. Explain the routing rules, middleware configurations, and load balancing strategies. Include examples of service-to-service communication patterns and failover mechanisms. Address monitoring and observability aspects of the service mesh.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Functionality
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the admin functionality including statistics collection, user management, and system monitoring capabilities. Detail the admin-only endpoints for retrieving system metrics, user analytics, and session statistics. Explain the role-based access control implementation, admin authentication requirements, and privilege escalation mechanisms. Include examples of admin dashboard data structures, reporting endpoints, and user administration workflows. Address security considerations for admin access, audit logging, and data privacy compliance for administrative functions.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the API Service business logic implementation. Document the session management system, CRUD operations for interpretation sessions, and interpretation request processing workflow. Detail the database schema, PostgreSQL integration, and data models for sessions and translations. Explain the integration with RabbitMQ for asynchronous job processing, admin functionality for statistics and user management, and JWT authorization middleware. Include API endpoint documentation, request/response schemas, error handling patterns, and security considerations. Provide examples of session lifecycle management and admin dashboard functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: JWT Authentication Middleware
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for JWT authentication implementation within the API Service. Detail the token generation process, signature verification, and expiration handling mechanisms. Document the middleware implementation for protecting routes, token extraction from headers, and user context injection. Explain the JWT_SECRET configuration, token refresh strategies, and security best practices. Include examples of protected route implementation, token validation flows, and error handling for invalid tokens. Address token storage considerations, CSRF protection, and logout mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Worker Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Worker Service asynchronous processing implementation. Detail the RabbitMQ integration, job consumption patterns, and background processing architecture. Document the Consul service registration and health checking, message queue configuration, and job processing workflows. Explain the scaling considerations, error handling strategies, and monitoring approaches. Include practical examples of job processing, queue management, and integration with the API service. Address performance optimization, retry mechanisms, and failure recovery patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Frontend Single Page Application. Document the web UI architecture, authentication flow implementation, webcam integration for sign language detection, and demo interface functionality. Detail the state management patterns, user interaction handling, and responsive design implementation. Explain the integration with backend services via Traefik routing, localStorage usage for offline functionality, and configuration management. Include examples of camera access, video processing, text output display, and user session management. Address browser compatibility, accessibility considerations, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the PostgreSQL database schema. Detail entity relationships, table definitions (users, sessions, translations), field specifications, and data types. Document primary keys, foreign key constraints, indexes, and database constraints. Explain the data validation rules, business logic enforcement, and data integrity measures. Include database schema diagrams, sample data examples, and data access patterns. Document the initialization process, schema evolution strategies, and data lifecycle management. Address performance considerations, query optimization, and backup/recovery procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Infrastructure Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the infrastructure components and deployment configuration. Detail the Docker Compose setup, service orchestration, networking configuration, and volume management. Document the Traefik reverse proxy configuration, Consul service discovery setup, RabbitMQ message broker configuration, and PostgreSQL database initialization. Explain the container networking, health checks, service registration patterns, and load balancing strategies. Include deployment topology diagrams, environment variable configuration, and infrastructure scaling considerations. Address monitoring, logging, and operational management of the infrastructure components.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all REST endpoints in the SignVue system. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for both authentication and business logic APIs. Detail the /auth endpoints (register, login, me), /api endpoints (sessions CRUD, interpretation-requests, stats), and their respective parameter specifications. Include authentication header requirements, error response formats, status codes, and rate limiting considerations. Provide practical curl examples, client implementation guidelines, and integration patterns. Address API versioning, backward compatibility, and deprecation policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations guide for the SignVue microservices system. Document production deployment strategies, environment configuration management, and scaling considerations. Detail monitoring and logging approaches, health check implementations, and service discovery patterns. Include troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues, performance optimization techniques, and maintenance procedures. Address security hardening, backup strategies, and disaster recovery planning. Provide operational runbooks, alerting configurations, and incident response procedures. Document CI/CD pipeline integration and automated deployment strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guide for contributing to the SignVue project. Document local development setup, code organization patterns, and development workflow. Detail the project structure, coding standards, testing strategies, and debugging approaches. Include guidelines for adding new microservices, extending existing functionality, and maintaining code quality. Document the build process, dependency management, and development environment configuration. Provide contribution guidelines, pull request procedures, and code review standards. Address development best practices, performance profiling, and optimization techniques.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# SignVue — Microservices Architecture Overview

## Project Introduction

SignVue is a demonstration project showcasing modern microservices architecture for real-time sign language recognition. The project covers business **REST APIs**, **JWT authentication service**, **web UI**, **RabbitMQ messaging**, **Consul service discovery**, **Traefik reverse proxy**, and **multi-container deployment**.

### Purpose Statement
The application provides real-time sign language recognition through webcam input, converting gestures into text and speech output using AI-powered processing.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Demonstrate microservices architecture with clear service boundaries
- Implement JWT-based authentication with role management (USER/ADMIN)
- Enable asynchronous processing through RabbitMQ message queues
- Showcase service discovery and load balancing with Consul and Traefik
- Provide a responsive web interface for user interaction

### Target Audience
- Software architects learning microservices patterns
- Developers interested in authentication and message queue architectures
- Students exploring real-time video processing integration

## Technical Architecture

The system follows a distributed microservices architecture with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph "Client Layer"
        Browser[Web Browser]
        Mobile[Mobile App]
    end
    
    subgraph "Edge Services"
        Traefik[Traefik Reverse Proxy]
        Frontend[Nginx Static Files]
    end
    
    subgraph "Authentication Layer"
        AuthService[Auth Service]
    end
    
    subgraph "Business Logic Layer"
        ApiService[API Service]
        WorkerService[Worker Service]
    end
    
    subgraph "Infrastructure"
        Consul[Consul Registry]
        RabbitMQ[RabbitMQ Broker]
        Postgres[PostgreSQL DB]
    end
    
    Browser --> Traefik
    Mobile --> Traefik
    Traefik --> Frontend
    Traefik --> AuthService
    Traefik --> ApiService
    ApiService --> Postgres
    ApiService --> RabbitMQ
    WorkerService --> RabbitMQ
    WorkerService --> Consul
    ApiService --> Consul
    AuthService --> Consul
    Frontend --> Traefik
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer**: Static Nginx hosting with React-like single-page application handling authentication, camera demo, and user interface interactions.

**Service Layer**: Three independent Node.js services communicating via HTTP and message queues:
- **Auth Service**: Handles user registration, login, and JWT token generation
- **API Service**: Manages business logic including session management and interpretation requests
- **Worker Service**: Processes asynchronous jobs from RabbitMQ queue

**Infrastructure Layer**: 
- **Consul**: Service registry and health checking
- **RabbitMQ**: Message broker for asynchronous processing
- **PostgreSQL**: Persistent data storage for users and sessions

### Design Patterns

The architecture implements several key patterns:
- **Microservices Pattern**: Each service has a single responsibility
- **API Gateway Pattern**: Traefik routes requests to appropriate services
- **Circuit Breaker Pattern**: Health checks prevent routing to unhealthy services
- **Event-Driven Architecture**: Asynchronous processing through message queues

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Frontend Application**
- `frontend/index.html` - Main HTML structure with navigation and demo sections
- `frontend/script.js` - Client-side JavaScript handling authentication, API calls, and UI interactions
- `frontend/config.js` - Configuration management for API base URLs

**Backend Services**
- `services/auth-service/src/index.js` - Authentication service with JWT token management
- `services/api-service/src/index.js` - Business logic service with session management
- `services/worker-service/src/index.js` - Asynchronous job processor

### Core Modules

**Database Schema** (`infra/init-db.sql`)
```mermaid
erDiagram
    USERS ||--o{ INTERPRETATION_SESSIONS : "creates"
    USERS ||--o{ TRANSLATIONS : "generates"
    INTERPRETATION_SESSIONS ||--o{ TRANSLATIONS : "contains"
    
    USERS {
        serial id PK
        varchar email UK
        text password_hash
        varchar role
        timestamptz created_at
    }
    
    INTERPRETATION_SESSIONS {
        uuid id PK
        varchar user_email
        varchar title
        text notes
        timestamptz created_at
    }
    
    TRANSLATIONS {
        uuid id PK
        integer user_id FK
        text source_text
        text target_text
        varchar lang_from
        varchar lang_to
        timestamptz created_at
    }
```

**Configuration Approach**
- `docker-compose.yml` - Centralized service orchestration with environment variables
- `services/*/Dockerfile` - Containerization for each service
- Environment-based configuration for different deployment stages

### External Dependencies

**Frontend Dependencies**
- Modern CSS custom properties for theming
- LocalStorage for offline functionality
- Fetch API for HTTP communication

**Backend Dependencies**
- Express.js for REST API development
- PostgreSQL driver for database operations
- JWT library for authentication tokens
- RabbitMQ client for message processing

### Integration Points

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Frontend
    participant Traefik
    participant AuthService
    participant ApiService
    participant RabbitMQ
    participant WorkerService
    
    User->>Frontend: Login/Register
    Frontend->>Traefik: /auth/* requests
    Traefik->>AuthService: Forward request
    AuthService->>AuthService: Validate credentials
    AuthService-->>Frontend: JWT token
    
    User->>Frontend: Start demo
    Frontend->>Traefik: /api/* requests
    Traefik->>ApiService: Forward request
    ApiService->>RabbitMQ: Publish job message
    ApiService-->>Frontend: 202 Accepted
    
    WorkerService->>RabbitMQ: Consume messages
    WorkerService->>WorkerService: Process job
    WorkerService-->>Console: Log results
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Authentication System**
- User registration with password hashing
- JWT token-based authentication
- Role-based access control (USER/ADMIN)
- Session management with automatic logout

**Real-time Demo**
- Webcam integration for gesture capture
- Live video preview with playback controls
- Text output display for recognized signs
- Responsive design for various screen sizes

**Asynchronous Processing**
- Message queue for job distribution
- Background processing without blocking UI
- Scalable worker service architecture

### Implementation Highlights

**Security Features**
- Password hashing with bcrypt
- JWT token verification
- CORS policy configuration
- Input validation and sanitization

**Performance Considerations**
- Database indexing for query optimization
- Connection pooling for PostgreSQL
- Message queue durability settings
- Health check endpoints for monitoring

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Authenticated: Successful login
    Authenticated --> DemoMode: Start demo
    DemoMode --> Processing: Send interpretation request
    Processing --> Result: Job completed
    Result --> DemoMode: Continue demo
    Result --> Authenticated: View history
    Authenticated --> Unauthenticated: Logout
    
    state Processing {
        [*] --> QueueJob
        QueueJob --> WaitAck: Message sent
        WaitAck --> Acknowledged: RabbitMQ ack
        Acknowledged --> [*]
    }
```

**Deployment Architecture**
- Multi-container Docker setup
- Service discovery with Consul
- Load balancing through Traefik
- Health monitoring and automatic failover

The project demonstrates a comprehensive microservices architecture suitable for educational purposes and can serve as a foundation for building production-ready distributed systems.

---

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [docker-compose.yml](docker-compose.yml)
- [index.html](frontend/index.html)
- [config.js](frontend/config.js)
- [script.js](frontend/script.js)
- [style.css](frontend/style.css)
- [auth-service/package.json](services/auth-service/package.json)
- [auth-service/src/index.js](services/auth-service/src/index.js)
- [api-service/package.json](services/api-service/package.json)
- [api-service/src/index.js](services/api-service/src/index.js)
- [worker-service/src/index.js](services/worker-service/src/index.js)
- [init-db.sql](infra/init-db.sql)
- [auth-service/Dockerfile](services/auth-service/Dockerfile)
- [api-service/Dockerfile](services/api-service/Dockerfile)
- [worker-service/Dockerfile](services/worker-service/Dockerfile)

</blog>
